message:
feat(storage,doctor): deferred blocked-cache refresh with stale-marker protocol

Move blocked-issues cache rebuild out of the main mutation transaction
to reduce lock contention on concurrent writes. The mutation transaction
now sets a `blocked_cache_state = stale` metadata marker and rebuilds
the cache in a separate follow-up transaction. All read paths
(get_blocked_ids, is_blocked, get_blockers, get_blocked_issues,
get_ready_issues) call ensure_blocked_cache_fresh() to lazily rebuild
when the marker is present.

Key changes:
- Add BlockedCacheRefreshPlan enum (Full vs Incremental) extracted from
  MutationContext, applied after the primary transaction commits
- Add with_connection_write_transaction() retry helper with exponential
  backoff for transient SQLite errors
- Add incremental cache helpers: load_direct_blockers_for_ids_impl,
  load_local_open_child_blockers_for_ids_impl, build_parents_by_child,
  expand_blocked_cache_component, filter_parent_child_edges_for_ids
- Doctor now detects stale blocked_cache_state as a recoverable anomaly
  and repairs it via ensure_blocked_cache_fresh() before falling back to
  full JSONL rebuild
- Switch fsqlite patches from pinned Git rev to local path overrides for
  faster iteration on engine fixes
- Minor clippy fixes: unused clone in create.rs, map_or_else in mod.rs,
  too_many_lines allow in defer.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"beads_rust-rdrp","title":"BUG: NOT NULL constraint fails on optional fields during insert","description":"# BUG: NOT NULL constraint fails on optional fields during insert

## Symptom
All E2E tests fail with:
```
Database error: NOT NULL constraint failed: issues.description
```

## Root Cause
Schema has: `description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''`
But INSERT statement passes `issue.description` directly as `Option<String>`.
When `None`, SQLite receives NULL which violates NOT NULL constraint.

## Fix
Convert `None` to empty string before INSERT for all `NOT NULL DEFAULT ''` fields:
- description
- design
- acceptance_criteria
- notes
- owner
- created_by
- close_reason
- closed_by_session
- source_system
- deleted_by
- delete_reason
- original_type
- sender

-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei","title":"Close the real-world reliability gap between green tests and catastrophic field failures","description":"## Problem
The current test story overweights clean-room behavior: fresh temp workspaces, scrubbed environment variables, a new `br` process per command, and narrow concurrency scenarios. Real users operate on long-lived `.beads` directories with existing SQLite sidecars, stale metadata, interrupted syncs, ambient env overrides, multiple agents/processes, and partial-failure history. That gap makes it possible for the full unit/e2e suite to pass while production users still hit catastrophic corruption, drift, and "doctor says OK but writes explode" outcomes.

## Goal
Make real-world workspace durability a first-class product property rather than a best-effort side effect of happy-path tests. The system must detect anomalous state early, recover safely and reversibly, preserve user data, and block releases that regress real-world reliability.

## Outcomes
1. A shared, executable definition of workspace health across SQLite, JSONL, metadata, WAL/SHM sidecars, dirty/export state, and derived caches.
2. A sanitized corpus of real-world failure states and a deterministic replay harness.
3. Crash, concurrency, filesystem, and long-lived-workspace tests that model actual agent usage rather than only fresh tempdir flows.
4. Recovery paths that are explicit, auditable, reversible, and validated by postconditions.
5. Release gates and operator playbooks that make reliability regressions hard to ship and easy to diagnose.

## Success Criteria
- We can reproduce the known classes of field failures inside tests instead of only discussing them.
- `br doctor`, startup/open, and write paths agree on whether a workspace is healthy, degraded, recoverable, or unsafe.
- Automatic recovery never silently destroys evidence or masks data loss.
- CI/release verification includes the real-world reliability suite, not just the current hermetic suite.

## Guardrails
- Do not paper over corruption with silent rebuilds that hide evidence.
- Preserve production realism; do not solve this by making tests even more synthetic.
- Keep the graph self-contained so future contributors can operate directly from beads without needing the original planning discussion.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:20.631398686Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:21.181161938Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["field-hardening","program","reliability"],"comments":[{"id":187,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Program charter: treat workspace durability as mission-critical. The core planning insight is that the current test harness proves isolated clean-room correctness much better than it proves long-lived production resilience. This epic exists to close that gap systematically, with executable invariants, realistic fixtures, recovery hardening, and release gates. All child beads should optimize for preserving user data, surfacing anomalies honestly, and making future regressions reproducible rather than anecdotal.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:21Z"}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","title":"Define workspace health contract and field failure taxonomy","description":"## Why
Today, different parts of the product use different implicit definitions of "healthy workspace." Startup/open logic, write paths, sync, and `doctor` can disagree about whether a given `.beads` state is fine, degraded, or corrupt. That guarantees ad hoc fixes and inconsistent operator outcomes.

## Scope
Define the canonical failure taxonomy and convert it into an executable health contract covering primary data, interchange data, sidecars, metadata, and derived state.

## Deliverables
- Named failure classes and severity levels.
- An invariant matrix for SQLite, JSONL, metadata, WAL/SHM/JOURNAL sidecars, dirty flags, export hashes, blocked cache, child counters, and last-touched context.
- A standard evidence bundle for real incidents.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Future reliability beads can cite concrete invariant names instead of vague "corruption" language.
- Health states can be tested directly.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:21.510428052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:22.491325690Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","invariants","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:21.510428052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":188,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Sequencing note: this track intentionally comes first. Without a stable vocabulary and contract, every later test or recovery change risks checking the wrong thing or normalizing a broken state.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","title":"Inventory and classify real-world workspace failure modes","description":"## Purpose
Create the canonical catalog of field-relevant failure modes instead of treating each new corruption report as a novel surprise.

## Include
- `beads.db` corruption classes: not-a-database, malformed schema, short reads, index mismatch, sidecar mismatch, duplicate schema entries, partially recoverable row-level corruption.
- JSONL issues: conflict markers, malformed lines, stale export, missing updates, mixed prefixes.
- Metadata/path drift: wrong db/jsonl target, missing or stale metadata, routed path confusion, ambient env overrides.
- Lifecycle interruption issues: crash during create/update/delete/sync/rebuild, partial temp-file cleanup, interrupted sidecar updates.
- Multi-actor issues: concurrent writers, interleaved reads+writes, multiple agents on one workspace, existing HOME/BEADS env state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every class has: symptom signature, likely root cause, observability surface, data-loss risk, and desired system response.
- The taxonomy is concrete enough to drive fixtures and tests in later beads.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:22.876629145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:23.787804922Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","incidents","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:22.876629145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":189,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Future-self note: do not let this devolve into a vague document. The point is to enumerate the exact workspace states we expect to survive, reject, quarantine, or rebuild from, using the failures users actually hit plus the states implied by the current code and troubleshooting docs.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:23Z"}]}

-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","title":"Define the incident bundle capture workflow for field failures","description":"## Purpose
Define the minimal reproducibility bundle we should gather the first time a field failure is reported so we stop losing crucial evidence.

## Bundle Contents
- failing command + stdout/stderr
- `br doctor --json`
- `metadata.json`
- `issues.jsonl`
- presence and hashes of `beads.db`, `beads.db-wal`, `beads.db-shm`, `beads.db-journal`
- environment overrides that affect discovery/path/output
- whether multiple agents/processes were active

## Acceptance Criteria
- The bundle is small enough to request quickly during incident response.
- The captured data is sufficient to classify the failure using the taxonomy from this track.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:25.615887382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:26.573923596Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["incident-response","ops","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:25.615887382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists because ad hoc bug reports waste time and collapse into speculation. -{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","title":"Expand crash, concurrency, and filesystem realism coverage","description":"## Why

Expand concurrency coverage from narrow lock-timeout scenarios to realistic multi-process and mixed-agent usage.

## Cover
- concurrent writers on one workspace

- multiple agents issuing different command families interleaved

+{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","title":"Encode an executable workspace health contract and invariant matrix","description":"## Purpose

+{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","title":"Define the incident bundle capture workflow for field failures","description":"## Purpose

+{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","title":"Create a sanitized fixture corpus for representative corrupt and drifted workspaces","description":"## Purpose

+{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","title":"Fix spurious primary-key errors after successful JSONL-only writes","description":"## Problem
During this planning session, `br create --no-db`, `br comments add --no-db`, and `br dep add --no-db` successfully persisted changes to JSONL and then still returned `DATABASE_ERROR` with `PRIMARY KEY constraint failed`. That makes automation believe the write failed even though state changed underneath it.

- create or update beads in `--no-db` mode against the current long-lived workspace
- observe the issue/comment/dependency present afterward
- observe the command exit non-zero with a bogus primary-key error after the successful write

 {"id":"beads_rust-rt7z","title":"Integrate rich output into quick commands (q, reopen, defer, undefer, count)","description":"## Commands: br q, br reopen, br defer, br undefer, br count

### Traffic Level: LOW-MEDIUM
Quick action commands that need simple confirmations.

### br q (quick create)
```
✓ Created beads_rust-abc1
  "Quick note about something"
```

### br reopen <ID>
```
✓ Reopened beads_rust-abc1
  Status: closed → open
```

### br defer <ID> [UNTIL]
```
✓ Deferred beads_rust-abc1
  Status: open → deferred
  Until: 2024-02-01 (7 days)
```

### br undefer <ID>
```
✓ Undeferred beads_rust-abc1
  Status: deferred → open
```

### br count [--status STATUS]
```
╭─ Issue Counts ──────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                     │
│  Total: 156                                         │
│  Open: 89    In Progress: 34    Closed: 33          │
│  Ready: 23   Blocked: 12   Deferred: 5              │
│                                                     │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

```rust
#[test]
fn test_q_creates_issue() {
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let output = render_q_result("Quick note", &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("Created"));
}

#[test]
fn test_reopen_shows_status_change() {
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let output = render_reopen_result("test-id", &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("closed"));
    assert!(output.contains("open"));
}

#[test]
fn test_defer_shows_until_date() {
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let until = chrono::Utc::now() + chrono::Duration::days(7);
    let output = render_defer_result("test-id", until, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("deferred") || output.contains("Until"));
}

#[test]
```

```bash
#!/bin/bash

log_pass "br reopen works"
```

 {"id":"beads_rust-rxg","title":"Config system (YAML + DB precedence + metadata.json)","description":"# Config System (YAML + DB + metadata.json)

## Purpose
Implement classic bd configuration semantics: **YAML config files + env vars + DB config table**, plus `.beads/metadata.json` for startup file paths. This is foundational for correct prefix handling, auto-import/flush flags, and routing behavior.

## Sources & Precedence (highest wins)
1. **CLI flags**
2. **Environment variables** (`BD_*`, plus select `BEADS_*`)
3. **Project config** `.beads/config.yaml` (walk up from CWD)
4. **User config** `~/.config/bd/config.yaml`
5. **Legacy user config** `~/.beads/config.yaml`
6. **DB config table** (runtime keys)
7. **Defaults**

## YAML-only Keys (startup settings)
Must live in config.yaml (cannot be stored in DB):
- `no-db`, `no-daemon`, `no-auto-flush`, `no-auto-import`, `json`
- `db`, `actor`, `identity`
- `flush-debounce`, `lock-timeout`, `remote-sync-interval`
- `git.*`, `no-git-ops`, `no-push` (read-only in br)
- `sync-branch` / `sync.branch` (ignored in br v1)
- `routing.*`, `directory.labels`, `external_projects`, `validation.*`, `hierarchy.max-depth`

## DB Config Keys (classic)
- `issue_prefix`
- `allowed_prefixes`
- `status.custom`, `types.custom`
- `import.missing_parents`
- `export.error_policy`, `auto_export.error_policy`, `export.retry_attempts`, `export.retry_backoff_ms`, `export.skip_encoding_errors`, `export.write_manifest`
- `max_collision_prob`, `min_hash_length`, `max_hash_length`

## metadata.json (startup file config)
File: `.beads/metadata.json`
Fields:
- `database` (DB filename, default `beads.db`)
- `jsonl_export` (JSONL filename, default `issues.jsonl`)
- `backend` (ignore dolt in br)
- `deletions_retention_days` (legacy, ignore)

If missing, use defaults. Legacy `config.json` should be auto-migrated to `metadata.json` on init (optional).

## Env Bindings
- `BD_*` (dots/hyphens => underscores)
- Legacy: `BEADS_FLUSH_DEBOUNCE`, `BEADS_AUTO_START_DAEMON`, `BEADS_IDENTITY`, `BEADS_REMOTE_SYNC_INTERVAL`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Correct precedence order for all config sources.
- YAML-only keys never stored in DB.
- metadata.json controls DB/JSONL paths before DB open.
- Env overrides honored.

## Tests
- Precedence tests (env overrides YAML overrides DB).
- metadata.json path override tests.
- YAML-only key handling tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:17:13.657995776Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T17:26:32.599293076Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T17:26:32.599293076Z","close_reason":"Implemented config precedence, startup resolution, metadata path handling, lock-timeout support, and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

### Purpose
This command helps maintain documentation for AI coding agents (like Claude, GPT) working on the project. The rich output helps humans verify what was generated.

### Testing Requirements

#### Unit Tests
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_check_result_formatting_exists() {
        let result = AgentsCheckResult {
            file_exists: true,
            has_context: true,
            is_outdated: false,
            sections: vec!["Overview".into(), "Commands".into()],
            missing_sections: vec![],
        };
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = format_check_result(&result, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("✓"));
        assert!(output.contains("AGENTS.md exists"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_check_result_formatting_missing() {
        let result = AgentsCheckResult {
            file_exists: false,
            has_context: false,
            is_outdated: false,
            sections: vec![],
            missing_sections: vec!["Overview".into()],
        };
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = format_check_result(&result, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("Missing") || output.contains("not found"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_update_progress_display() {
        let steps = vec![
            UpdateStep::Completed("Extracted project context".into()),
            UpdateStep::Completed("Listed commands".into()),
            UpdateStep::InProgress("Writing file".into()),
        ];
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = format_update_progress(&steps, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("✓"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_json_mode_check_output() {
        let result = AgentsCheckResult {
            file_exists: true,
            has_context: true,
            is_outdated: false,
            sections: vec!["Overview".into()],
            missing_sections: vec![],
        };
        let ctx = OutputContext::json();
        let output = format_check_result(&result, &ctx);
        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&output).unwrap();
        assert!(parsed["file_exists"].as_bool().unwrap());
    }
}
```

```rust
#[test]
fn test_agents_check_no_file() {
    let dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    init_test_project(&dir);
    // Don't create AGENTS.md

    let result = run_agents_check(&dir);
    assert!(result.is_ok());

}

#[test]
fn test_agents_add_creates_file() {
    let dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    init_test_project(&dir);

    let result = run_agents_add(&dir);
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    assert!(dir.path().join("AGENTS.md").exists());
}

#[test]
fn test_agents_update_modifies_file() {
    let dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    init_test_project(&dir);
    // Create initial AGENTS.md
    std::fs::write(dir.path().join("AGENTS.md"), "# Old content").unwrap();

    let result = run_agents_update(&dir);
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    let content = std::fs::read_to_string(dir.path().join("AGENTS.md")).unwrap();
    assert!(content.len() > 20); // Should have more content now
}
```
